title: B1 Listening - Part 1 - Test 20
instructions: For each question, listen to the recording and choose the correct answer: A, B or C.

items.context: You hear two students talking about a charity race.
items.audio_script: Man: Have you seen the message about the charity race? I wrote down at the school gate, but I cannot find the message now. || Woman: That was the original plan, but the organisers are giving out race numbers there. [1]We need to meet beside the sports hall instead[/1]. || Man: Thanks for telling me. I would have waited at the school gate and missed everyone. || Woman: No problem. I will get there a few minutes early, and I will text you if the organiser changes anything again before we leave.
items.question: Where should they meet?
items.explanation: They should meet beside the sports hall.

items.context: You hear two friends preparing for an overnight stay.
items.audio_script: Girl: Have you packed for the overnight stay? I was going to take a sleeping bag. Is that still right? || Boy: It was on the first list, but beds and pillows are provided, but guests need their own washing things. [2]You need to bring a towel instead[/2]. || Girl: Good thing I asked. I nearly closed my bag without it. || Boy: Put it in now and check again before you leave. The organiser said we must be ready on time tomorrow, because the group cannot wait for anyone who forgets something important.
items.question: What should the speaker take?
items.explanation: The speaker needs to take a towel.

items.context: You hear two friends making Sunday plans.
items.audio_script: Boy: Are we still going to play golf on Sunday? I told my family I would be home later. || Girl: That was our first idea, but the golf course is too wet after the rain. We could go to visit a castle, though the castle is closed until next month. [3]We are going to go climbing instead[/3]. || Boy: That sounds good. Shall we meet near the station? || Girl: Yes, at four. I will book our places now, so there should not be a problem when we arrive.
items.question: What are they going to do?

items.context: You hear two people talking about a coach journey.
items.audio_script: Woman: I need to check the time for the coach to the airport. I wrote down eight o'clock, but I am not sure it is correct. || Man: It changed this morning because the road outside the station is being repaired. [4]It starts at quarter past eight now[/4], and we should arrive ten minutes earlier. || Woman: I am glad I asked. I would have arrived at the wrong time. || Man: I will send you a reminder later. Bring everything you need, because there will not be time to go back home first.
items.question: What time does the coach leave?
items.explanation: The coach now leaves at quarter past eight.

items.context: You hear two people discussing a printer.
items.audio_script: Man: How are you getting on with your printer? I heard there was a problem with it. || Woman: I first thought there was no paper, and my brother wondered whether it would not connect to the computer. But the shop checked it carefully this morning. [5]It has run out of ink[/5]. || Man: Can they fix that before the homework deadline tomorrow? || Woman: They think so. Now they know what is wrong, it should be ready tomorrow afternoon. That is a relief, because I need to use it this weekend.
items.question: What is wrong with the printer?
items.explanation: The printer has run out of ink.

items.context: You hear two friends planning a visit to the library.
items.audio_script: Girl: How are you travelling to the city library on Saturday? I thought you were going by car. || Boy: I planned to, but the car park is closed this weekend. I considered going by bus, but the bus route has changed and takes too long. [6]I am going by underground instead[/6]. || Girl: That sounds sensible. What time will you arrive? || Boy: Just before eleven, if everything is on time. I checked the timetable online, so we have plenty of time, and I will message you when I leave home.
items.question: How will they get to the library?

items.context: You hear two people choosing dessert.
items.audio_script: Boy: Did you buy the chocolate cake for dessert? I saw one yesterday and thought it would be suitable. || Girl: No. I looked at the chocolate cake, and I nearly chose ice cream, but the cake is too rich and it is too cold outside for ice cream. [7]I bought apple pie in the end[/7]. || Boy: That sounds like the right choice. It is more useful than the other things. || Girl: I hope so. I kept the receipt in case I need to change it later.
items.question: What will the speaker order?
